name: Cross Sends the Assessment
description: After the monastery meeting, General Cross has changed the strategic assessment of the Wastes from enemy territory to recovering Grid infrastructure. He wants the revised command assessment delivered to Thorn before standard channels, because Thorn will understand it and act on it rather than filing it.
accept_text: He is writing when you arrive. Not the command-brief shorthand he uses for orders — full sentences, careful. He finishes a paragraph before he looks up. 'I spent three hours in a room with Commander Serel and I came out of it holding a changed understanding of what we've been doing for thirty years.' He doesn't say this as a complaint. He says it the way you say a thing that is both difficult and correct. 'The Wastes are not enemy territory. They are a healing mechanism for an infrastructure that predates both our nations. Both our nations have been fighting over the surface layer of something that neither of us built and that neither of us has the right to claim.' He picks up the assessment. 'I'm changing the formal designation. Wastes — what we call the Wastes, the Aetherion calls the Resonance Fields — are being reclassified in my command assessment from enemy-contested territory to: Grid recovery zone, active infrastructure, non-national.' He holds out the document. 'This will go through standard channels and it will take three months and fifteen signatures and by the time it reaches the people who need to read it the reading moment will have passed.' He looks at you. 'Thorn has been sitting in the middle of it for thirty years. He's been watching the Abomination coordination patterns change, the crystal pulse intervals shift, the behavior of the Hollow evolve. He knows something is happening and he knows the current strategic framework doesn't explain it.' He holds out the document. 'Take it to him. Let him read it. Then tell him: the assessment is going through channels as well, but I wanted him to have it first, from someone who came out of a three-hour meeting understanding what he's been looking at.'
complete_text: Thorn reads the assessment standing up, which is how he reads things that don't let him sit down. He reads it twice. He sets it on the map table and looks at the Wastes markers — all the red ink, all the patrol lines, all the years of marking a territory that has been, in the assessment he is now holding, reclassified as something else entirely. 'Grid recovery zone.' He says it quietly. 'Active infrastructure.' He looks at the crystal under his skin — the slow blue pulse that is the same frequency as the network's construction wave, that has been beating in his cells for two years in exact time with something he is only now being told is real. 'I've been sitting in the middle of it.' He says it to himself. 'Every report I've sent about the Abomination coordination, the pulse intervals, the Hollow behavior. I've been describing a healing process and calling it an enemy.' He is quiet for a long time. 'How long did Serel and Cross spend in that hall.' Not a question exactly. Then: 'Tell Cross — I'll coordinate with his field assessment on the eastern approach. The sectors where the pulse rate is highest — I've been treating those as high-alert areas. I'm reclassifying them as high-recovery areas.' He picks up the assessment and puts it in the command journal. 'And tell him: thank you for sending it through someone who could get here faster than the standard chain.' He pauses. 'Three hours.' He says it again. 'Thirty years and it took three hours.' He looks at the map. 'The woman in Hive District Two. The letter I've been trying to write.' He straightens. 'I think I know what to put in it now.'
